response.title: DeepSeek-R2 benchmark results
response.body: Existing post about DeepSeek-R2 benchmark results, posted 2 days ago.
response.author: another_user

comments.body:
Latest benchmark results indicate DeepSeek-R2 continues to show impressive performance across multiple evaluation suites:

- **MMLU**: 85.3% (5-shot)
- **HumanEval**: 72.1% (pass@1)
- **GSM8K**: 89.2% (5-shot)
- **HellaSwag**: 85.7% (0-shot)

These results place DeepSeek-R2 among the top open-weight models in its class, particularly excelling in mathematical reasoning and coding tasks. The model also shows improved performance on multilingual benchmarks compared to its predecessor.

comments.body:

These results represent a significant step forward in reasoning and coding capabilities compared to previous versions. The model demonstrates strong multilingual performance and improved instruction following.

The benchmark was conducted under standardized evaluation conditions using the same methodology as previous DeepSeek releases. Full details are available in the official technical report.

comments.body: The European Commission has just released detailed enforcement guidelines for the AI Act, clarifying compliance timelines, risk classification procedures, and supervisory authority roles. Key points: high-risk AI systems must meet conformity assessments by August 2026, with some provisions applying earlier. National authorities will coordinate through the European AI Office. The guidelines also outline transparency requirements for generative AI and provide a template for fundamental rights impact assessments. Organizations should start mapping their AI systems against the new classification framework now.
